ee Attribute vs, Attribute Often, a character uses one of his attributes 
Ina contest with another character, instead of Using it to affect an inanimate object. For ex ample, in an armawrestling match, characters tse their strength to try to overcome each other. This is called an attribute vs. attribute contest. Roll a die for each character. Add each character's attribute o his die-oll. The higher total wins Example: Joc Rand Bob arm wrestle, Joe Rs stength is 6; he rolls. a 13, fora total score (of 19, BobY’s strength is 12; he rolls a 9 for 8 score of 21. BobY wins The gamemaster may modify one or both attributes to reflect an advantage or disadvan: tage. For example, if Bob were weak with hunger, you might reduce his strength by 3 belore rolling Important Note: Remember that low rolls are ‘normally good, but ina contest between two characters, high rolls are good. 
Increasing Attributes (Optional) Now, generally we feel that you shoulda’ let players increase attributes. The whole point is that skills are learned and can be increas- 
cd, while attributes are innate bodily thingies, find cart, However, there's always some star. {ass in the group who points out that fhe goes to the gym every day for six months and exe. ses unt all he can doi le ina poo of sweat tnd twitch, he can increase his strength Pre Sonally, we think unnecessary physical exer. tion is contrary to the laws of nature, but it's at least philosophically posible fr this to hap- pen. So here's how to deal with smarases: 'N any time, a character may submit are questo The Computer orto superior in his Service group for additional taining andior a 
AUTATH youR 
personal development program fr himself and his clone family. The bes time to submit such 2 request is ater an especially successful mis Sion, or ater having rendered The Computer or Alpha Complex some special service. Strength, endurance, agility, and dexterity are usually improved by special training Courses offered by the Armed Forces. After Several months of rigorous taining (dodging shells, sparing with kungducbols, etc), a whole élone family may have improved one atibute by one point. Or maybe not. Or maybe they got worse, Or lost the function of both legs, Or maybe they suffer a nervous breakdown when they hear a loud noise ‘Moxie, chutzpah, and mechanical attibute improvement programs are available through HPD and Mind Control's Education Depart ment, where conventional instructional pro- trams are supplemented by RNA injection, drug therapy sleep training, and various other dangerous and. unreliable educational technologies (Ofna character will come back from a six- week program with a one-point improvement in his moxie, chutapah, or mechanical at tribute, Ithappens al the time. Rumors of ac- cidental deaths inthe Educational Department are greatly exaggerated, It isnot true that ex tensive loss of memory often accompanies biochemical education, And i's just not true thatthe improvements often disappear after a few months — along with various skills and motor functions, There's nothing to. worry about. Trust us “There Is no training aailable for the power attribute, Except trough the black market, and Psion and other secret societies. Which don't exist, and would certainly be treasonous if they did, 1OUs and. large cash payments. are gratefully accepted in these non-existent tran- ing programs which either increase the power attribute by one point, or result in. the rrysterious disappearance ofthe character and his clone family, or in various other sinister side effects ‘Occasionally R&D offers spectacular tain- ing and developmental programs involving ex perimental technologies: cyborging, brain- microchip interfaces, genetic. engineering reconstnictive surgery, psychological condi- tioning, mutant engineering and so on. Often 
Citizens will be volunteered for these programs asa way of granting a request for training and personal development. Incredible. im provements are possible — attributes may in ‘crease dramatically. There are certain risks, of course, Aside from unappetizing, cosmetic alterations (microprocessors protruding from the skull, extra arms and legs, massive pros- thetics, monstrous deformities, and so.on), the side effects of these experimental programs ‘may alter or erase memory and motor func- tions, or the character's personality Incidentally, citizens are often assigned 10 training or personal development programs whether they request them or not, This may bbe The Computer's way of thanking a citizen for a job well done, Or telling a citizen he should get on the bafl, Or punishing... er rehabilitating a citizen for & treasonous act. 
Interpreting Rolls (Optional) Sometimes you want to know how well a player did something — whether he sueceed- fd particularly well, or bungled particularly badly, Use the number rolled as an indication of performance, We don’t want t0 get t00 bogged down in detall here; suffice ito say thata roll which is much lower than the at tribute is a spectacular success, and a. roll ‘which is much higher isa spectacular failure. Example: Sent on a mission Into the Outdoors to recover Old Reckoning technology and return ito Alpha Complex, the PCs encounter {tribe of primitives who live in and worship 43 derelict "Carz-a-Poppin’ car wash, Con- ‘inced that this giant mechanism holds some fantastic importance to Alpha Complex, Joe- R-BOB-3 frantically attempts to use his 
mechanical aptitude of 13 to repair it. His player rolls — a "1"! The gamemaster rules that rot only does Joe-R-BOB get this ancient device to creak into motion, but that it begins 
Cleaning everything about it with ay abandon, Primitives run shrieking, covered with suds and 
foamy glant Urushes whip down rom the ceil ing and bowl PCs along the floor; water and soap spray everywhere, Joe-R is made into a tou by the primitives. Or maybe burned a the Stake. You decide
